title: Knowledge-in-Context: Towards Knowledgeable Semi-Parametric Language Models

structured.summary: The paper presents Knowledge-in-Context (KiC), a semi-parametric language model architecture that integrates external knowledge to augment a parametric text-to-text model, improving its performance on diverse NLP tasks. KiC demonstrates superior zero-shot performance on over 40 tasks compared to larger fully-parametric models, enabled by its efficient knowledge selection mechanism from an external memory consisting of six different knowledge types.

strengths.evidence: KiC outperforms larger models like GPT-NeoX-20B and OPT-30B on zero-shot tasks with only 770M parameters, achieving 39.4% on the MMLU benchmark.
The approach uses an external memory with six knowledge types to enhance adaptability and performance without extensive model scaling.

weaknesses.evidence: The paper references similar semi-parametric approaches without clearly distinguishing KiC's unique contributions.
KiC is mainly compared against fully-parametric models, with little comparative analysis against other semi-parametric models.

questions.question: How does the knowledge selector determine which knowledge to retrieve?
questions.motivation: Detailed understanding of the knowledge selection mechanism is crucial for assessing its effectiveness.

questions.question: What are the practical implications of task improvements?
questions.motivation: It is important to know if task improvements translate to real-world applications.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of novelty clarity.. The paper references similar semi-parametric approaches without clearly distinguishing KiC's unique contributions
critique_points.point_002: Incomplete evaluation comparisons.. KiC is mainly compared against fully-parametric models, with little comparative analysis against other semi-parametric models
